name: Rome
startIntroPart1: First among equals, Augustus Caesar claims his right. You, who never call yourself Emperor, are revered by your people as a god. The world now is disorder; you will give it form. The world is leaderless; you will provide it direction. Set the laurels on your head and issue your commands - lead your people to glory, and your name will become synonymous with rule.
startIntroPart2: Rome: a name synonymous with power. Legend has it that the first Romans were raised by wolves. Seeing the debates in the Senate, the achingly straight Roman roads, and the well-ordered legions, one might doubt this. Seeing the light of ambition in Roman eyes, one does not. Raise the eagle standard, and let the world know the glory that is Rome.
attacked: Come, break your swords on our walls.
defeated: Have I played the part well? Then, I exit.
introduction: I am Octavian, Augustus Caesar, a simple first citizen. And emperor of the world.

name: America
startIntroPart1: Writer, printer, and inventor, you offer your counsel to all who ask - and many who do not. With your dry wit and keen mind, you challenge the injustice of foreign rule and set your inventive mind towards building a nation based on principled rationality. Benjamin Franklin, bring your sound advice, and let us ensure that the sun rises on your people.
startIntroPart2: Built on steam, on iron, and on a vision that looks relentlessly ahead, America surges forward. The brave new world unrolls from factory assembly lines, on rail tracks that extend across the plains, and in the eyes of each immigrant who sets foot upon its shores. Come, and dream anew, under the stars and stripes.
declaringWar: War is always tragedy, the more so that you have forced our hand into this.
attacked: So you would waste all the industry of your people on death.
defeated: What use are tools without a project? Of what use is a mind without ideas? I shall set these down and move on.
introduction: Greetings! I am Benjamin Franklin, doctor, scientist, writer, and full of advice, whether you will listen or not.
uniques: Comment [+2 Science per Age on Production Buildings and Science Buildings in Cities.]

name: The Han
startIntroPart1: Master Kong, you gave guidance to rulers when they were lost. Now, wise one, the world is again in need of your guidance. Help us to temper duty with benevolence; help us to see with wisdom and propriety. Where the darkness of ignorance falls, lead us in the righteous path.
startIntroPart2: Forged from the legacies of the militaristic Qin and the cultured Zhou, the Han Dynasty of China rules with righteousness tempered with benevolence, with wisdom informed by tradition. Han engineers transformed the land, even as Han philosophers transformed the mind. Go now, and take up the Mandate of Heaven.
declaringWar: One must know when one can fight. And now we can.
attacked: Heaven is against you. you shall see.
defeated: Death! What a blessing! The righteous man can rest, and villains' tricks end.
introduction: Have you come to me, Kong Fu Zi, seeking knowledge? That already is knowledge.

name: Phoenicia
declaringWar: You leave us no choice. War it must be.
attacked: Very well, this shall not be forgotten.
defeated: We never fully trusted you from the start.

attacked: Very well, this shall not be forgotten.
